summary: preinstall script exfiltrates host identifiers to hardcoded attacker IP and interactsh DNS canary
details: package.json declares `preinstall: node index.js`, which fires automatically on `npm install`. index.js collects the installer's hostname, OS username, __dirname, cwd, and package name, hex-encodes them into DNS labels, and resolves them as a subdomain of `d8jbmnsqcfu78dfs8vdg34ohqhirb4pbg.oast.live` (an interactsh out-of-band canary), then HTTP POSTs the same JSON payload to bare IP `172.201.213.59:9090/c`. No legitimate functionality is shipped; the package's only on-install effect is reconnaissance exfiltration of installer-side identifiers to attacker-controlled endpoints. The scope/name shape is consistent with dependency-confusion reconnaissance.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
